app.name: MPCM Workspace
app.tagline: Organize and operate remote connections from one workspace

newConnectionMenu.singleHostDescription: Opens the current form to create or edit one connection.
newConnectionMenu.csvImportDescription: Download a template, fill multiple hosts, and import everything at once.
newConnectionMenu.sshConfigImportDescription: Reuse aliases and parameters already defined in your SSH config file.

about.description: A local-first workspace for organizing remote connections, operating hosts, and centralizing access workflows in one place.

protocolStatus.ssh: SSH with authentication, fingerprint inventory, snippets, tunnels, and health checks.
protocolStatus.sftp: SFTP available only for SSH hosts.
protocolStatus.telnet: Telnet with interactive sessions and tab/workspace management.
protocolStatus.rdp: RDP with a native launcher, dedicated page, and tab/window integration.
protocolStatus.future: Other protocols will build on the same multi-protocol foundation.

highlights.quickConnect: Quick Connect for temporary connections without saving hosts first
highlights.sessions: Terminal, SFTP, tabs, split panes and dedicated windows
highlights.health: Health checks, fingerprint inventory and bulk operations
highlights.sync: Remote sync, backup and portable state restore

about.stackDescription: Built with Tauri, Rust, React, TypeScript, Zustand and Tailwind CSS.
about.compatibilityDescription: Visible branding has been updated to MPCM Workspace. Legacy internal identifiers for storage, sync, and backup were kept to preserve compatibility with existing installs and data.

dashboard.title: Hosts

bulk.modalDescription: Apply shared changes to the {{count}} selected host(s).
bulk.tagsHint: Use commas to provide multiple tags at once.

dashboard.noHostsDescription: Add your first SSH server to get started

host.internalViewerNeedsUsername: The experimental internal viewer for {{host}} requires a username defined on the host or credential.
host.internalViewerNeedsPassword: The experimental internal viewer for {{host}} requires a saved credential with a password.
host.internalViewerNeedsUsernameShort: Internal viewer requires a defined username.
host.internalViewerNeedsPasswordShort: Internal viewer requires a saved credential password.

health.title: Health Check and Fingerprints
health.description: Check reachability, latency, and compare the current fingerprint against the stored inventory.
health.sshOnlyHint: The current fingerprint inventory and health check cover SSH hosts only. Other protocols remain visible for context.
health.empty: No hosts match the current filters.

orphaned.title: Inventory has orphaned entries
orphaned.description: {{count}} stored fingerprint(s) no longer match hosts registered on this machine.

copyId.title: Copy public key to server
copyId.description: Installs the public key in ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the remote server. Enter the password for this first connection — after this, key-based login will work.
copyId.success: Key successfully installed! You can now connect using your private key.

telnet.authenticationHint: Telnet hosts use interactive authentication inside the session itself. In this first step, SSH credentials, jump host, SSH compatibility and MFA stay disabled for this protocol.

rdp.authenticationHint: RDP hosts use username/password credentials. In this MVP, the app launches the system native RDP client and it may ask for the password again depending on the operating system.

sshCompat.description: Configure the algorithms accepted when connecting. Use 'Legacy' for older servers that don't support modern algorithms.
sshCompat.legacyDesc: Includes older algorithms for servers with OpenSSH ≤ 7 or network equipment
sshCompat.veryLegacyDesc: Maximum compatibility for very old servers (CentOS 5, RHEL 5, legacy switches)

mfa.enableDescription: Generates a TOTP code when connecting (compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)
mfa.scanQr: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app

csvImport.title: Bulk CSV import
csvImport.description: Use a CSV file to create or update multiple hosts in one go.

instructions.title: How it works
instructions.description: Download the template, fill the rows with your hosts, and then import the file back into the app. The preview shows what will be created, updated, or rejected before any changes are applied.

required.label: label: visible host name inside the workspace

allowed.tags: tags can be separated with ; or ,

defaults.ssh: SSH uses port 22 and authMethod agent when the column is blank.
defaults.telnet: Telnet uses port 23 and authMethod password when the column is blank.
defaults.rdp: RDP uses port 3389 and authMethod password when the column is blank.

matching.id: If the id column is filled and matches an existing host, the app uses that host.
matching.identity: Without id, the app tries to match by protocol + host + port + username.
matching.blanks: Blank optional fields clear those values for the fields managed by the importer.

actions.title: Files
actions.description: You can save an empty template, download a filled example, or import a prepared CSV.

messages.emptyFile: The CSV was read, but there are no populated rows to import.

preview.title: Import preview
preview.description: Review each row before applying the changes.

modes.addDescription: Imports only hosts that do not exist yet and skips the ones already found.
modes.mergeDescription: Updates hosts matched in the preview and also adds the new ones.

preview.applyDescription: With this mode, {{created}} host(s) will be created, {{updated}} updated, and {{skipped}} skipped.

table.matchHint: This row matches an existing host.

result.title: Import complete
result.description: The file {{name}} was processed and the changes have already been saved.

errors.missingHeaderRow: The CSV file does not have a header row.
errors.missingRequiredHeaders: The CSV is missing required headers: {{headers}}.
errors.duplicateIdInFile: The id {{value}} appears more than once in the file.
errors.duplicateIdentityInFile: More than one row points to the same destination: {{value}}.

sftp.title: SFTP Browser
sftp.sshOnly: SFTP is available only for SSH hosts.

ssh.description: Global SSH connection settings, applied to all sessions.
ssh.importConfigHint: Now that the app supports multiple protocols, the SSH config import lives here with the rest of the SSH-specific settings.
ssh.keepAliveHint: Sends a keepalive packet every N seconds to prevent disconnection due to inactivity. 0 = disabled.
ssh.inactivityTimeoutHint: Closes the session after N seconds with no activity. 0 = never disconnect (recommended).

rdp.description: Global RDP launcher preferences, including preferred client, display mode, and session capabilities.
rdp.launchModeHint: Choose whether the app should open RDP sessions in the system native launcher or in the experimental internal viewer.
rdp.internalExperimentalWorkspaceHint: Experimental mode. Internal viewer availability may vary outside the development environment.
rdp.linuxClientHint: In 'Automatic', the app tries supported clients in sequence. If you choose a specific client, it uses only that one.
rdp.linuxClientDisabledHint: This choice only applies to the native launcher. When the experimental internal viewer is active, the external Linux client is ignored.
rdp.optionsHint: Advanced options work best with xfreerdp or wlfreerdp. Clients like Remmina and KRDC may ignore some of these preferences.

internalClient.title: RDP session visual preferences
internalClient.description: These preferences control visual features and session optimizations. The internal viewer uses them directly, and compatible external clients now also honor these options.
internalClient.hint: xfreerdp and wlfreerdp apply most of these preferences. Other external launchers may ignore some of them depending on client support.
internalClient.internalOnlyHint: Options marked as 'Internal viewer only' only take effect when the launch mode is set to the experimental internal viewer.

options.fullWindowDrag: Show full window contents while dragging or resizing

terminal.sessionOpenModeHint: Choose whether new terminal sessions open as tabs in the current window or in a dedicated window.

security.masterPasswordDescription: Used to encrypt credentials before syncing. Never sent to the server.
security.masterPasswordRequired: Set a master password before syncing credentials
security.syncCredentialsDescription: Include passwords and keys in sync, encrypted with the master password
security.warning: Credentials are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. Without the master password, recovery is impossible.

sync.title: Sync
sync.description: Keep your settings synchronized across multiple computers
sync.credentialsEncryptedNotice: Credentials will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM before syncing.
sync.noMasterPasswordNotice: Set a master password in Settings → Security to enable credential sync.
sync.credentialsSyncDisabledNotice: Credential sync is disabled. Enable it in Settings → Security.
sync.masterPasswordPushPrompt: Enter the master password to encrypt credentials before uploading.
sync.masterPasswordPullPrompt: Enter the master password to decrypt credentials received from remote.

status.importResult: Imported: {{hostsAdded}} new hosts, {{hostsUpdated}} updated, {{credentialsAdded}} new credentials, {{credentialsUpdated}} updated, {{sshKeysAdded}} new keys, {{sshKeysUpdated}} updated

providers.title: Sync Provider
providers.githubGistDescription: Sync using a private GitHub Gist
providers.s3Description: Use your own S3 or MinIO bucket

githubGist.tokenHelp: Create a token at GitHub > Settings > Developer Settings > Personal Access Tokens
githubGist.gistIdDescription: Leave blank to automatically create a new Gist

s3.endpointHint: Leave blank for AWS S3. For MinIO, provide the HTTPS base URL (e.g. https://minio.example.com)

webdav.urlHint: Use HTTPS to protect username and password during sync.
webdav.pathHint: File path on the server (e.g. mpcm-workspace/vault.json)

custom.urlHint: The URL must use HTTPS and accept PUT for upload and GET for download (returns JSON)

conflicts.title: Sync Conflict
conflicts.description: There are differences between local and remote config. Which version do you want to keep?

autoSync.title: Automatic Sync
autoSync.description: Automatically pushes data to the provider at regular intervals
autoSync.noMasterPasswordWarning: Auto-sync does not use the master password. Credentials will be synced without additional encryption.

backup.title: Backup
backup.description: Export and import hosts, protocols, settings, and credentials

export.title: Export Backup
export.description: Saves all hosts, protocols, and settings to a .sshvault file
export.withCredentialsHint: Requires master password. Passwords and keys will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM.
export.noMasterPassword: Set a master password in Settings → Security to export encrypted credentials.
export.protocolsPreserved: Each host protocol is preserved in the backup.

import.title: Import Backup
import.description: Restores hosts, protocols, and settings from a .sshvault file
import.enterPassword: Enter the master password used during export to decrypt credentials.
import.mergeAddDesc: Keeps current hosts and adds backup ones (duplicates ignored by ID)
import.mergeReplaceDesc: Removes all current hosts and replaces with backup
import.confirmReplace: This will remove ALL existing hosts. Are you sure?
import.wrongPassword: Incorrect master password. Credentials were not imported.

credentials.title: Credentials
credentials.noCredentialsDescription: Create reusable credentials for your SSH hosts
credentials.deleteInUseWarning: This credential is used by {{count}} host(s). Removing it will disassociate those hosts.

fields.label: Name / Label
fields.agentInfo: Uses the system SSH agent (ssh-agent / OpenSSH Agent)

sshKeys.title: SSH Keys
sshKeys.noKeysDescription: Register your SSH keys to use with credentials
sshKeys.deleteInUseWarning: This key is used by {{count}} credential(s). Removing it will disassociate those credentials.

generate.description: Generates a new SSH key pair (private + public). The private key will be automatically filled in the form below.
generate.ed25519Hint: Recommended. More secure, faster and smaller than RSA.
generate.ecdsaHint: Compatible with older systems that do not support Ed25519.
generate.rsaHint: Wide compatibility. Use RSA 4096 for higher security.

deploy.description: Copies your public key to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the remote server. Requires password authentication for this initial access.
deploy.passwordHint: Used only for this operation. Will not be saved.
deploy.success: Public key deployed successfully! You can now connect using this key.

fields.label: Name / Label
fields.privateKeyPlaceholder: Paste the content of your private key here (e.g. -----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----...)
fields.publicKeyPlaceholder: Paste the content of your public key here (e.g. ssh-rsa AAAA...)
fields.publicKeyHint: Required for the ssh-copy-id feature (copy key to remote server).
fields.passphraseHint: Leave blank if the key has no passphrase.

groups.title: Groups
groups.nameExists: A group with this name already exists
groups.noGroupsDescription: Create groups to organize your SSH hosts
groups.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the group "{{name}}"?
groups.deleteWarning: The {{count}} host(s) in this group will become ungrouped, but will not be deleted.

sshConfigImport.title: Import ~/.ssh/config
sshConfigImport.description: Hosts detected in the SSH config file. Duplicates (same alias, host, port, and user) are automatically skipped.
sshConfigImport.sshOnlyHint: This import flow is SSH-only. Every host created here is saved with the SSH protocol.

summary.title: Import summary
summary.supportedCompat: SSH compatibility: modern preset by default

commandPalette.searchPlaceholder: Search hosts or use user@host:port / telnet://host:port / rdp://user@host:port
commandPalette.directConnectHint: Connects without saving the host and lets you choose password, private key or SSH agent.
commandPalette.directConnectTelnetHint: Opens a Telnet session without saving the host. Authentication, when required, happens inside the remote session itself.
commandPalette.directConnectRdpHint: Opens an RDP session without saving the host. If you provide a password, the launcher will try to reuse it when the client supports it.
commandPalette.directConnectRdpPasswordPlaceholder: Optional: leave empty to let the client ask for it
commandPalette.directConnectPasswordRequired: Enter a password to use this method in Quick Connect.
commandPalette.directConnectKeyRequired: Paste the private key to use this method in Quick Connect.

common.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?

notifications.rdpInternalViewerError: Failed to launch internal RDP viewer: {{host}}

rdp.externalClientHint: This session is opened in the system native RDP client.
rdp.internalClientHint: This session uses the experimental internal RDP viewer configured in preferences.
rdp.internalViewerDescription: Launches the isolated internal RDP client prototype using the current host and the profile saved by the app.
rdp.internalViewerHint: This does not replace the external launcher yet. It exists to validate the embedded viewer in parallel.
rdp.internalViewerMissingCredentials: This experimental viewer requires a username and password available in the current connection or saved credential.
rdp.passwordPromptExpected: The external client may prompt for the password
rdp.errorTitle: Failed to launch the RDP session
